description: The Broadcasting Board of Governors (BBG) was the US government agency responsible for supervising and funding non-military, international broadcasting by the United States from 1994 to 2018. It oversaw VOA Persian, Radio Farda (via Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty), and other international broadcasting services targeting audiences in authoritarian states. In 2018, Congress reorganised the BBG into the [US Agency for Global Media (USAGM)](/orgs/us-agency-for-global-media), which assumed all its functions and responsibilities.
editorial_notes: Predecessor to USAGM (renamed/restructured 2018). Supervised VOA Persian and Radio Farda.
